title: MatchBundle
description: One match, complete: Domains A-G plus the pre-rendered per-team storyStats the Hero Layer shows. This is the only artifact a match route fetches (AD-4). Every aggregate, distribution, percentage and total is a field — the App never sums or averages (AD-5).

schemaVersion.title: MatchBundleSchemaVersion
schemaVersion.description: Stamped from /contract/version.json. One global integer, declared exactly once (AD-2).

momentum.$comment: PROVISIONAL series shape (OQ-5 / AR-17). The concrete shape lands in Story 1.8 via the AD-14 change flow, with a schemaVersion bump and regenerated fixtures in the same commit. The KEY contract is final in v1: required, never omitted, never [], and null triggers the App's empty state.
momentum.title: Momentum

goalkeeping.title: GoalkeepingBlock
goalkeeping.description: One entry per goalkeeper who appeared, both teams, ordered home team first.

players.title: PlayerRecords
players.description: One entry per player with minutes, both teams, ordered home team first then by shirt number.

TeamRef.title: TeamRef
TeamRef.description: Identity of one of the two teams in this match.

name.title: TeamName
name.description: Team name exactly as the cover scoreline prints it. A proper noun, not a translated label (AD-7).

MinuteStamp.title: MinuteStamp
MinuteStamp.description: A point on the match clock. Ordering is by (minute, stoppageMinute) — the roving-tabindex keyboard navigation over pitch markers depends on it.

Formation.title: Formation
Formation.description: Formation as the lineup page prints it, locale-neutral (AD-7). Thirteen distinct values were observed across the 104-report corpus, from 3-4-1-2 to 5-4-1.

LineupEntry.title: LineupEntry
LineupEntry.description: One named player in a team sheet. playerId is mandatory on every entry — EXPERIENCE.md links each lineup name to its Player Profile.

name.title: LineupEntryName
name.description: Player name as the lineup block prints it (given name plus upper-case surname).

goals.title: LineupEntryGoalMinutes
goals.description: Minutes at which this player scored. Own goals are NOT listed here; they appear in metadata.goals attributed to the benefiting team.

GoalRecord.title: GoalRecord
GoalRecord.description: One goal in the match, in chronological order. Own goals are attributed to the BENEFITING team here (teamId) while scorerPlayerId names the player who put it in — and they are excluded from shot maps entirely (AD-6).

ownGoal.title: GoalOwnGoal
ownGoal.$comment: PMSR prints no own-goal marking anywhere in the 104-report corpus (verified 2026-07-22). v1 therefore always emits false; a source that distinguishes own goals is an AD-14 change request.

penalty.title: GoalPenalty
penalty.description: An in-play penalty kick. Shoot-out conversions are never goals — see EventTables.shootoutAttempts.

MatchMetadata.title: MatchMetadata
MatchMetadata.description: Domain A. Team ordering is explicit and load-bearing: DESIGN.md's two-team accent rule assigns accent A to the home / first-listed team, so it must be encoded, never inferred from key order. `score` is the final score as the cover prints it — after extra time when extra time was played, otherwise after 90; the per-period breakdown lives in `knockoutScore`.

matchNumber.title: MatchNumber
matchNumber.description: Match number within the tournament, 1-104, as printed on the cover stage line.

group.title: MatchGroup
group.description: Group letter for a group-stage match, null for every knockout tie.

venue.title: Venue
venue.description: Venue name as the cover prints it. Sixteen distinct venues across the corpus.

date.title: MatchDate
date.description: Calendar date of the match, venue-local, ISO 8601 (AD-7).

kickoff.title: Kickoff
kickoff.description: Kick-off instant, ISO 8601 venue-local WITH its UTC offset, so the App can render either local or absolute time without a venue timezone table.

goals.title: Goals
goals.description: Every goal in the match, chronological. The Momentum Timeline's goal markers come from here, never from the momentum series.

StoryStats.title: StoryStats
StoryStats.description: The five Hero Layer tiles for one team (FR-21). Exactly five fields — Story 2.4 renders exactly five tiles. All five are aggregates, so AD-5 requires them precomputed and shipped, never summed in the browser.

MomentumSample.title: MomentumSample
MomentumSample.description: One sampled point on the momentum axis. EXPERIENCE.md's aria-valuetext announces the minute plus both teams' values, which is exactly this shape.

TeamKeyStatistics.title: TeamKeyStatistics
TeamKeyStatistics.description: Domain B, one team. Nineteen fields, matching the Match Summary - Key Statistics page row for row; compound rows such as 'Total Passes (Complete)' and 'Defensive Pressures Applied (Direct Pressures)' are split into their two numbers rather than stored as a formatted string (AD-7). `sprintDistance` is the page's second distance row, 'Zone 4 - Low Speed Sprinting: 20-25 km/h'.

KeyStatisticsBlock.title: KeyStatisticsBlock
KeyStatisticsBlock.description: Domain B for both teams. contestedPossession is a match-level third share: the page prints possession as home / contested / away, and the three sum to 100, so the middle value cannot be derived from the two team values and is stored once here.

InPossessionPhases.title: InPossessionPhases
InPossessionPhases.description: The eight in-possession percentages from the Phases of Play page. These are INDEPENDENT per-phase rates, not a partition: they do not sum to 100 and must never be normalized or treated as slices of a whole. Verified against the corpus — Germany's eight values in PMSR-M74-GER-V-PAR sum to 124. All eight are stored; none is derived (AD-5).

OutOfPossessionPhases.title: OutOfPossessionPhases
OutOfPossessionPhases.description: The nine out-of-possession percentages from the Phases of Play page. Independent per-phase rates, not a partition — they do not sum to 100.

PossessionSplitMetres.title: PossessionSplitMetres
PossessionSplitMetres.description: A distance measured separately in and out of possession.

DefensiveBlockDistribution.title: DefensiveBlockDistribution
DefensiveBlockDistribution.$comment: These are the SAME three numbers as phasesOutOfPossession.highBlock / .midBlock / .lowBlock — the Phases of Play page is their only source. They are surfaced again here because Story 2.10's #pressing section renders block height as its own concept. The pipeline must emit them equal to the phase values; pipeline/tests/test_fixtures.py asserts that equality so the two copies cannot drift.
DefensiveBlockDistribution.description: Percentage of defensive time spent at each block height. Independent rates, not a partition — they do not sum to 100.

ShotEvent.title: ShotEvent
ShotEvent.$comment: teamId is the SHOOTING player's team. Own goals are flagged ownGoal: true, excluded from shot-map rendering, and attributed to the benefiting team in metadata.goals (AD-6).
ShotEvent.description: One attempt at goal, plotted on the shots pitch map. Shoot-out attempts never appear here — they would break marker-count self-validation and Story 2.7 never plots them.

expectedGoals.title: ShotExpectedGoals
expectedGoals.$comment: PMSR prints xG only as a team total on the Key Statistics page; the shots event table has no xG column (verified across all 104 reports, 2026-07-22). v1 therefore emits null per shot. A per-shot xG source is an AD-14 change request.

ShootoutOutcome.title: ShootoutOutcome
ShootoutOutcome.description: Result of one penalty shoot-out attempt.

ShootoutAttempt.title: ShootoutAttempt
ShootoutAttempt.$comment: teamId is the TAKING player's team. A shoot-out attempt is never a ShotEvent and never a GoalRecord.

order.title: ShootoutOrder
order.description: 1-based position in the shoot-out sequence, across both teams.

CrossEvent.title: CrossEvent
CrossEvent.$comment: teamId is the CROSSING player's team.
CrossEvent.description: One open-play cross, plotted on the crosses pitch map.

PassNetworkNode.title: PassNetworkNode
PassNetworkNode.description: One player's average position on the pass-network map. x and y are extracted from the page, never derived from the edges (Story 1.14). `involvement` is the total passes this player was involved in, made plus received — a derived aggregate and therefore a field (AD-5), which DESIGN.md encodes as node size.

PassNetworkEdge.title: PassNetworkEdge
PassNetworkEdge.description: A directed passing relationship between two players. DESIGN.md maps volume onto the edge-weight ramp.

volume.title: PassNetworkEdgeVolume
volume.description: Number of completed passes along this edge. Always at least 1 — a zero-volume edge is simply absent.

ReceivingEvent.title: ReceivingEvent
ReceivingEvent.$comment: teamId is the RECEIVING player's team.
ReceivingEvent.description: One offer to receive or movement to receive. The type discriminator is what lets the two pitch maps share a single event shape — Story 2.9 renders #offers-to-receive and #movement-to-receive from the same array.

movementType.title: ReceivingMovementType
movementType.description: Offer movement category, null when the report does not classify this event.

contestType.title: DefensiveActionContestType
contestType.description: Sub-category, present only when actionType is possession-contest; null otherwise.

EventTables.title: EventTables
EventTables.description: Domain D. Every table is a flat array carrying an explicit teamId per row, so one shape serves both the pitch panel and the accessibility data table — there are no 'lite' variants (EXPERIENCE.md). An empty array means zero events of that kind; null means the report does not carry that data at all.

shootoutAttempts.title: ShootoutAttempts
shootoutAttempts.$comment: PMSR carries only the aggregate shoot-out line on the cover ('(Switzerland win 4-3 on Penalties)') and no per-attempt table anywhere in the report — verified against all four shoot-out ties in the corpus (M74, M75, M88, M96) on 2026-07-22. v1 therefore emits null, which is distinguishable from an empty array; the aggregate lives in metadata.knockoutScore.shootoutScore. A per-attempt source is an AD-14 change request.

CompletionCounts.title: CompletionCounts
CompletionCounts.description: A complete/incomplete pair plus its total. The total is stored, not derived, so a report whose printed total disagrees with its parts becomes a visible deviation rather than a silent correction.

GoalkeeperDistribution.title: GoalkeeperDistribution
GoalkeeperDistribution.description: Domain E distribution. Category counts and the total are both stored, so their internal consistency is checkable.

CrossDeliveryTypeCounts.title: CrossDeliveryTypeCounts
CrossDeliveryTypeCounts.description: Counts across the six cross delivery types plus their total.

involvementTimeline.title: GoalkeeperInvolvementTimeline
involvementTimeline.description: Involvements bucketed along the match clock, as the Goalkeeping Involvement page's timeline chart plots them.

CornerSideCounts.title: CornerSideCounts
CornerSideCounts.description: Corners of one delivery type, split by the side they were taken from. The two sides sum to the total.

OfferMovementCounts.title: OfferMovementCounts
OfferMovementCounts.description: One player's offers, split across the six movement types.

PlayerOutOfPossession.title: PlayerOutOfPossession
PlayerOutOfPossession.description: Domain G, out-of-possession half. tacklesMade and tacklesWon are the two halves of the page's 'Tackles Made / Won' column.

PlayerPhysical.title: PlayerPhysical
PlayerPhysical.description: Domain G, physical half. Distances are metres, as the Physical Data page prints them. The speed zones are the page's own bands: distanceZone1 is 0-7 km/h, distanceZone2 is 7-15, distanceZone3 is 15-20, distanceZone4 is 20-25, distanceZone5 is 25+. highSpeedRuns counts Zone 3 runs; sprints counts Zone 4 and 5 runs.

PlayerRecord.title: PlayerRecord
PlayerRecord.description: Domain G, one player. Every field the Expert Layer's per-player tables show is here — there is no reduced variant.
